description: Sample graph data for testing falkor-irac without real judgment PDFs.

cases.name: Sanjay Chandra v. CBI
cases.summary: Laid down three considerations for bail in economic offences: gravity of charge, likelihood of fleeing justice, and likelihood of tampering with evidence.

cases.name: Arnesh Kumar v. State of Bihar
cases.summary: Directions on arrest in offences punishable with imprisonment up to seven years. Police must apply mind before arrest; Magistrate must be satisfied before authorising detention.

cases.name: Kalyan Chandra Sarkar v. Rajesh Ranjan
cases.summary: A bail application after rejection by a court of coordinate jurisdiction requires fresh grounds or changed circumstances.

cases.name: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
cases.summary: Article 21 right to life includes right to live with dignity. Procedure established by law must be fair, just, and reasonable.

sections.title: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence
Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail
When police may arrest without warrant

issues.text: What are the relevant considerations for grant of bail in economic offences?
Whether arrest is mandatory in offences punishable with imprisonment up to seven years?
Whether a subsequent bail application is maintainable after rejection by a coordinate bench?

precedent_relationships.proposition: Bail considerations: gravity of charge, flight risk, evidence tampering
